title: Skinners Sense Of Snow

jokes.text: I'm more worried about Al's jacket. How many stations can you get on that thing?
jokes.setup: Sports commentators making jokes about each other's wardrobe
jokes.punchline: Comparing Al's loud jacket to a TV antenna/radio receiver
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good economy - the jacket-as-antenna metaphor is visual and specific

jokes.text: All right. Listen. I am sick of your jokes about the wardrobe! You people can dress yourselves!
jokes.setup: Commentators making wardrobe jokes
jokes.punchline: Defensive overreaction to harmless ribbing
jokes.explanation: Works as escalation from the previous joke but fairly standard defensive reaction

jokes.text: The Cirque de Purée. We've had tickets since Septembre.
jokes.setup: Family discussing circus plans
jokes.punchline: Food pun on Cirque du Soleil, with pretentious French pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Marge's character trying to be cultured, plus the pretentious French month name adds layers

jokes.text: Oh! I missed one of Jerry's zingers. Now I'll never catch up.
jokes.setup: Homer being forced to leave TV for circus
jokes.punchline: Treating TV banter like complex serialized content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating throwaway TV jokes like important plot points he needs to follow

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. That's way better than fun and excitement.
jokes.setup: Marge praising circus's 'whimsy and wonder'
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically preferring boring-sounding alternatives
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer sarcasm, perfectly delivered deadpan response to Marge's pretension

jokes.text: As French Canadians, they don't believe in refunds... or exploiting animals for entertainment.
jokes.setup: Explaining French Canadian circus philosophy
jokes.punchline: Pairing financial policy with animal rights as equal concerns
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of mundane business policy with noble artistic principles

jokes.text: Oh! I wanted to see 'em fire a gorilla out of a cannon.
jokes.setup: Discussion of circus not exploiting animals
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately wanting the most extreme animal exploitation possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing the point and wanting the most ridiculous spectacle imaginable

jokes.text: Enjoy the show. For one day, we shall die.
jokes.setup: Standard circus welcome
jokes.punchline: Existential dread delivered as cheerful greeting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of cheerful circus patter with matter-of-fact mortality reminder. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: I cannot get the lid off my jar of rainbows!
jokes.setup: Circus performance setup
jokes.punchline: Treating abstract concept (rainbows) as physical jarred commodity
jokes.explanation: Delightfully absurd concept that captures pretentious art-circus aesthetic perfectly

jokes.text: Just smash it open!
jokes.setup: Whimsical circus performance about opening jar of rainbows
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally practical solution to artistic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing artistic subtlety and going straight to brute force

jokes.text: Oh, you can do it if your heart is pure- or... purée.
jokes.setup: Inspirational circus message about pure hearts
jokes.punchline: Forced pun callback to circus name
jokes.explanation: Weak forced pun that breaks character moment for cheap wordplay callback

jokes.text: They always pick the guy with the wires.
jokes.setup: Circus performer flying through air
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about obvious circus mechanics
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on circus illusions, fits Bart's cynical perspective

jokes.text: Watching those women is giving me ideas.
jokes.setup: Marge watching exotic circus performers
jokes.punchline: Marge having sexual thoughts about acrobatic positions
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of Marge's usual prudish character, delivered with characteristic innocent tone

jokes.text: Mesdames et messieurs, it appears the Cloud Goddess is ripe with rain babies.
jokes.setup: Announcing incoming storm
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic/ridiculous meteorological description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sideshow Mel pretentiousness - turning weather into flowery theatrical announcement

jokes.text: Oh, no, you don't! I paid full price for this freak show!
jokes.setup: Circus performers running from storm
jokes.punchline: Homer demanding entertainment value regardless of safety
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness - prioritizing entertainment over performer safety

jokes.text: and then melting in the summer.
jokes.setup: Weather forecast of escalating storm conditions
jokes.punchline: Adding obvious long-term outcome as if it's part of storm warning
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax joke - building up dramatic weather only to state the obvious

jokes.text: Arr! Help. I was tied here by teenage pirates.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain appears hunkered down in storm
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation for why he's tied up
jokes.explanation: Absurd but character-appropriate situation for the Sea Captain

jokes.text: And it's open season on savings at Springfield Menswear, which is closed.
jokes.setup: Radio announcement about open/closed establishments
jokes.punchline: Store advertising being open while actually being closed
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection joke about contradictory retail messaging

jokes.text: Lenny says we've got the day off! Yippee!
jokes.setup: Homer at work hearing about snow day
jokes.punchline: Homer treating coworker's comment as official policy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer wishful thinking - taking any excuse to avoid work

jokes.text: Oh, why does that always happen?
jokes.setup: Homer tries to make snow angel
jokes.punchline: Homer's body shape creates inappropriate-looking impression in snow
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Homer's body shape creating unintended results, with his oblivious reaction

jokes.text: Uh, hey, Lou, could you shake out the last few drops for me? Yeah, no problem, Chief. Seems like a waste of coffee, though.
jokes.setup: Chief asking Lou for help
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's about urination, with Lou's confused response
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection joke with bathroom humor, saved by Lou's misunderstanding

jokes.text: But you've kept intact my Cal Ripken-like streak of school 'openage.'
jokes.setup: Skinner praising students for showing up
jokes.punchline: Making up pompous word 'openage' for simple concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner pretentiousness - creating fake academic word and comparing school attendance to baseball

jokes.text: my Cal Ripken-like streak of school 'openage'
jokes.setup: Skinner proud of attendance despite storm
jokes.punchline: Making up word 'openage' and comparing to baseball streak
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - bureaucratic pride in made-up achievement with awkward neologism

jokes.text: Caucus, caucus, caucus. Caucus, caucus, caucus.
jokes.setup: Teachers calling emergency meeting
jokes.punchline: Teachers chanting 'caucus' like a ritualistic song
jokes.explanation: Absurd take on union organizing, making political process seem cultish

jokes.text: But we'll have the last laugh on those Johnny-come-not-lies.
jokes.setup: Skinner dealing with absent teachers
jokes.punchline: Mangling 'Johnny-come-lately' into nonsensical insult
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner malapropism showing his tendency to mangle common phrases

jokes.text: Ha-ha! Next time get a DVD!
jokes.setup: Students complaining about poor quality movie
jokes.punchline: Nelson's suggestion, followed by revelation it already IS a DVD
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup with Nelson's typical know-it-all attitude backfiring immediately

jokes.text: Albinos virtually invisible
jokes.setup: News report about blizzard conditions
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of albinos blending into snow
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny throwaway line about weather endangering specific population

jokes.text: Sold off to billionaire Montgomery Burns... in a veritable orgasm of poor planning.
jokes.setup: News report about missing snowplows
jokes.punchline: Kent's overwrought sexual metaphor for bad government
jokes.explanation: Classic Kent Brockman over-the-top reporting with inappropriate sexual language

jokes.text: This is terrible! How will the kids get home? I don't know. Internet?
jokes.setup: Chalmers asking about getting kids home in blizzard
jokes.punchline: Skinner's completely illogical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Skinner's disconnect from practical reality

jokes.text: I'm doing a puzzle with Grandmama, and she'll finish without me.
jokes.setup: Students complaining about being stuck
jokes.punchline: Üter's sweet but pathetic concern about puzzle
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that's both endearing and slightly sad

jokes.text: I can cut a trail through the snow. I'm part Eskimo.
jokes.setup: Nelson offering to help escape
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous claim about heritage as qualification
jokes.explanation: Good Nelson moment - his typical bragging with completely made-up qualification

jokes.text: I don't care if you're Kristi Yamaguchi. No one leaves the building.
jokes.setup: Nelson claiming to be part Eskimo
jokes.punchline: Skinner's oddly specific Olympic figure skater reference
jokes.explanation: Great random reference that shows Skinner's weird cultural touchstones

jokes.text: I don't care if they're kissing Kristi Yamaguchi. You're not going home!
jokes.setup: Students wanting to see TV show
jokes.punchline: Skinner repeating Yamaguchi reference in different context
jokes.explanation: Callback works by showing Skinner stuck on the reference, though less effective than original

jokes.text: I don't care if they're kissing Kristi Yamaguchi. You're not going home!
jokes.setup: Kids wanting to see Itchy and Scratchy kiss episode
jokes.punchline: Skinner reusing Kristi Yamaguchi reference inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Skinner's obsession with Yamaguchi reference makes no sense in new context

jokes.text: My car, your roof. It's only fair.
jokes.setup: Ned complaining about Homer sawing off his roof
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted logic about fairness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - making destructive selfishness sound reasonable

jokes.text: Sure, you did. 'Mr. Plow.' You're wearing the jacket right now.
jokes.setup: Homer denying he had a plow business
jokes.punchline: Ned pointing out obvious evidence Homer is wearing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to classic episode with visual evidence of Homer's denial

jokes.text: I think I know my own life, Ned.
jokes.setup: Ned providing clear evidence of Homer's plow business
jokes.punchline: Homer stubbornly denying obvious reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stubbornness - refusing to acknowledge clear evidence about his own past

jokes.text: If you don't eat your relish, you're not getting any mayonnaise.
jokes.setup: Skinner rationing awful emergency food
jokes.punchline: Treating condiments like a proper meal sequence
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation of Skinner's authoritarianism applied to terrible food rationing

jokes.text: Screw this. The rest of you can stay here like dorks, but I'm going home.
jokes.setup: Nelson deciding to escape
jokes.punchline: His immediate face-plant into snow drift
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with Nelson's bravado immediately punished by physical reality

jokes.text: Well, I hope you've all learned something from Nelson's 'headstrongedness.'
jokes.setup: Nelson stuck headfirst in snow
jokes.punchline: Skinner making up word that literally describes Nelson's position
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Skinner moment - creating pompous vocabulary that accidentally describes the visual gag perfectly

jokes.text: What would Superintendent Chalmers do? Skinner! Eh, that's no help.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to think of leadership advice
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing Chalmers only yells his name
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware moment that acknowledges the running gag while being genuinely insightful

jokes.text: Nope. Too dangerous. We're all gonna sit tight and reminisce about candy bars
jokes.setup: Soldier suggesting escape plan
jokes.punchline: Skinner choosing nostalgia over action during war
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - avoiding risk through nostalgic distraction, explaining his current behavior

jokes.text: Get back to the candy bar!
jokes.setup: Soldier telling story about beach and girl
jokes.punchline: Sergeant obsessed with candy bar details over attractive woman
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing military obsession with mundane details over obvious interests

jokes.text: That elephant ate my entire platoon.
jokes.setup: Skinner's Vietnam flashback triggered by current situation
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation connecting past trauma to present
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd backstory that explains Skinner's current behavior while being completely ridiculous

jokes.text: Actually there are. Five, 10, 15, 20- Uh, can you two share a hook?
jokes.setup: Bart claiming not enough coat hooks for everyone
jokes.punchline: Martin methodically proving him wrong, then accommodating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin moment - his literal-mindedness deflating dramatic rebellion

jokes.text: I think we hit something. I hope it's Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge hitting something while driving
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual hope for neighbor violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer dark humor - casual hoping for harm to Flanders

jokes.text: I'm just kidding! Hey, you're all right.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he hoped they hit Flanders
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate backpedal when consequences appear
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Homer's cowardly social navigation

jokes.text: use this bucket next to Bart's head.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining bathroom arrangements for the night
jokes.punchline: Placing toilet bucket next to specific student's sleeping area
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about terrible emergency arrangements

jokes.text: Yeah. As a tribute to Princess Di. And Dodi. Because these days, princesses-
jokes.setup: Ned claiming he wrote a love song
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly connecting it to recent royal tragedy
jokes.explanation: Dated topical reference showing Ned's awkward cultural commentary

jokes.text: Is that some sort of plush novelty? Well, here's a scouring pad. It's just as good.
jokes.setup: Child asking for comfort toy
jokes.punchline: Skinner's completely inappropriate substitute suggestion
jokes.explanation: Great Skinner moment showing his complete disconnect from child psychology

jokes.text: Well, here's a scouring pad. It's just as good
jokes.setup: Student asking for stuffed rabbit for comfort
jokes.punchline: Offering abrasive cleaning tool as cuddly substitute
jokes.explanation: Hilariously inappropriate substitute that shows Skinner's complete misunderstanding of comfort

jokes.text: It's cold and hurt-y.
jokes.setup: Child given scouring pad instead of stuffed animal
jokes.punchline: Child's simple description of obviously bad substitute
jokes.explanation: Sweet child logic describing obviously terrible comfort object

jokes.text: I know it looks like the path to freedom. But one collapse, and presto- you've got a snow casket.
jokes.setup: Skinner stopping kids from using Bart's escape tunnel
jokes.punchline: Darkly dramatic description of tunnel dangers
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner dramatics with morbid imagery to control students

jokes.text: Defying orders, eh? Well, I see you Scotsmen are thrifty with courage too.
jokes.setup: Willie refusing to destroy tunnel
jokes.punchline: Skinner's ethnic stereotype insult about Scottish cowardice
jokes.explanation: Skinner's authoritarian escalation with ethnic stereotyping showing his nastiness

jokes.text: That's the last time you'll slap your Willie around.
jokes.setup: Willie quitting after Skinner insults him
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual innuendo in Willie's defiant exit line
jokes.explanation: Classic accidental innuendo that works both literally and sexually

jokes.text: You want the pee bucket on your head?
jokes.setup: Skinner stuck and asking for help
jokes.punchline: Bart threatening disgusting punishment
jokes.explanation: Escalating gross-out humor as power dynamics reverse

jokes.text: I'm writing all your names on the detention list in my mind.
jokes.setup: Skinner being physically abused by students
jokes.punchline: Threatening imaginary punishment while completely powerless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - clinging to bureaucratic authority when he has no actual power

jokes.text: I always knew I would die caked in something.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge trapped in snow-filled car
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific death premonition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Homer having weirdly specific but vague death expectations

jokes.text: Wait. Let's just leave it on... till we forget our troubles
jokes.setup: Ned suggesting they turn off engine to avoid fumes
jokes.punchline: Homer suggesting suicide by carbon monoxide
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer suggestion for avoiding problems through self-harm

jokes.text: I can't write this. It's a grammatical nightmare.
jokes.setup: Students being forced to write things on chalkboard
jokes.punchline: Martin's academic standards preventing him from writing poorly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character moment - his pedantry preventing him from participating in rebellion

jokes.text: After all the times I've done it... my wrist sounds like a cement mixer.
jokes.setup: Skinner being forced to write repetitive lines
jokes.punchline: Implication of chronic masturbation causing wrist problems
jokes.explanation: Adult humor disguised as innocent comment about repetitive punishment

jokes.text: I told you no one can climb a rope. It's physically impossible
jokes.setup: Students attempting rope climbing
jokes.punchline: Skinner declaring normal gym activity impossible
jokes.explanation: Skinner's defeatist attitude projecting his own physical limitations as universal truth

jokes.text: What part of didi mau don't you understand?
jokes.setup: Skinner having Vietnam flashbacks while being tortured
jokes.punchline: Using Vietnamese phrase inappropriately with students
jokes.explanation: Skinner's trauma bleeding into present situation inappropriately

jokes.text: Quiet, Principal Spinner!
jokes.setup: Nelson addressing captive Skinner
jokes.punchline: Mocking name change suggesting Skinner is spinning/confused
jokes.explanation: Simple name-change insult that fits the power reversal situation

jokes.text: 'Underachiever and proud of it.' How old is this thing?
jokes.setup: Bart reading his permanent record
jokes.punchline: Reference to his classic early seasons catchphrase as outdated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging the show's own evolution and dated elements

jokes.text: Underachiever and proud of it.' How old is this thing?
jokes.setup: Bart finding his permanent record
jokes.punchline: Referencing his own early series catchphrase as outdated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's own evolution and Bart's character development

jokes.text: 'Lisa is an outstanding student with a slight tendency toward know-it-all-ism'? That's not even a word!
jokes.setup: Lisa reading her school record
jokes.punchline: Proving the point by correcting the made-up word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - immediately demonstrating the very trait she's being criticized for

jokes.text: Twenty-five thousand dollars a year! Whoa! He's a millionaire!
jokes.setup: Kids discovering Skinner's salary
jokes.punchline: Kids thinking $25k is enormous wealth
jokes.explanation: Good child perspective joke about money scale comprehension

jokes.text: Let's see. He's 40 years old, times 25 grand. Whoa! He's a millionaire!
jokes.setup: Nelson doing math on Skinner's lifetime earnings
jokes.punchline: Terrible math leading to wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Escalating the math joke with Nelson's confident but wrong calculation

jokes.text: Plus, in the summer, he paints houses! He's a billionaire!
jokes.setup: Kids calculating Skinner's wealth
jokes.punchline: Adding minor summer job and jumping to billions
jokes.explanation: Further escalating the absurd wealth calculations

jokes.text: If I were a billionaire, why would I be living with my mother?
jokes.setup: Students thinking he's a billionaire
jokes.punchline: Skinner's logical counter-argument revealing embarrassing truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner logic that simultaneously defends and embarrasses himself

jokes.text: Godspeed, Nibbles. Godspeed.
jokes.setup: Skinner sending hamster for help
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic farewell to pet rodent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner dramatics applied to absurd situation with classroom pet

jokes.text: If you get me out of this, there's a hall monitor position coming open in the spring.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to negotiate with Willie
jokes.punchline: Offering pathetic reward that caused the current problem
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Skinner learning nothing and offering the same terrible incentive

jokes.text: I spit on your monitors! I know. That's why the position's available.
jokes.setup: Willie rejecting hall monitor offer
jokes.punchline: Skinner revealing the position exists because Willie sabotaged it
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation revealing the circular logic of their conflict

jokes.text: I spit on your monitors!
jokes.setup: Skinner offering hall monitor position
jokes.punchline: Willie's dramatic rejection of school authority
jokes.explanation: Good Willie moment - overly dramatic response to mundane school position

jokes.text: Homer, wake up! The car's filling with- I know. Ranch dressing.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to wake Homer about danger
jokes.punchline: Homer's dream state interpretation of snow as condiment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Homer moment connecting danger to food through dream logic

jokes.text: Just like the one that saved Ezekiel!
jokes.setup: Ned seeing hamster ball
jokes.punchline: Random Biblical reference that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned moment - making up biblical connection to modern situation

jokes.text: Just like the one that saved Ezekiel!
jokes.setup: Finding hamster ball for escape
jokes.punchline: Ned's biblical reference to hamster rescue story
jokes.explanation: Ned's character-appropriate biblical reference to unlikely hamster ball salvation story

jokes.text: You're the camel! Hey, this car's got cruise control.
jokes.setup: Homer still in desert fantasy while driving
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to mundane car feature observation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist transition from fantasy to mundane reality

jokes.text: It's happening. My horoscope was right!
jokes.setup: Car crash about to happen
jokes.punchline: Ned crediting astrology despite being Christian
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - devout Christian believing in horoscopes during crisis

jokes.text: Not Huck Finn! I spent hours crossing out the sass-back!
jokes.setup: Books being burned during student takeover
jokes.punchline: Skinner's censorship efforts being destroyed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Skinner moment revealing his petty censorship and attachment to his 'work'

jokes.text: Do you have air bags? No! The church opposes them for some reason!
jokes.setup: Car about to crash
jokes.punchline: Absurd religious opposition to safety features
jokes.explanation: Absurd Ned explanation combining safety concern with arbitrary religious prohibition

jokes.text: No! The church opposes them for some reason!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about airbags during crash
jokes.punchline: Ned's vague religious opposition to safety features
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned moment - blindly following church doctrine even when it's absurd and life-threatening

jokes.text: You did it, Nibbles! Now, chew through my ball sack.
jokes.setup: Skinner thanking hamster for rescue
jokes.punchline: Innocent request with unfortunate sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Accidental innuendo works but relies on cheap double entendre

jokes.text: There is, sir. Ah! Then I'm happy.
jokes.setup: Chalmers demanding explanation for chaos
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' immediate satisfaction without hearing explanation
jokes.explanation: Great Chalmers moment showing his administrative detachment and easy satisfaction

jokes.text: if there's one thing I'm good at... it's pretending things didn't happen.
jokes.setup: Bart addressing the episode's events
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on sitcom reset button
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about how sitcoms return to status quo despite major events

jokes.text: Boy, that salt really ate through the car.
jokes.setup: Family leaving school after rescue
jokes.punchline: Revealing consequences of salt rescue method
jokes.explanation: Mild callback joke about unintended consequences of rescue method

jokes.text: Ignore her, Effendi. We have each other
jokes.setup: Marge making donkey noises
jokes.punchline: Homer's romantic address to donkey using Middle Eastern title
jokes.explanation: Absurd ending with Homer romancing donkey while family looks on

tmdb_overview: A snowstorm traps the students of Springfield Elementary inside, with them overthrowing Principal Skinner when he uses his Army skills to control them. Meanwhile, Homer tries to rescue the children–using Flanders' car.
